Control Description: Information security policy and topic-specific policies shall be defined, approved by management, published, communicated to and acknowledged by relevant personnel and relevant interested parties, and reviewed at planned intervals and if significant changes occur.
Evidence Location: Information Security Policy (NGU-POL-5.1) / Information Security Risk Management Policy (NGU-POL-IRM01)
Comments: Information security policy framework documented and reviewed in the tabletop assessment.

Control Description: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated according to the organisation needs.
Comments: Security roles incl. Information Asset Owners defined; see NC-002 re consistent execution.

Control Description: Conflicting duties and conflicting areas of responsibility shall be segregated.
Comments: No segregation-of-duties policy in the reviewed set; requires process/records verification.

Control Description: Management shall require all personnel to apply information security in accordance with the established information security policy, topic-specific policies and procedures of the organisation.
Comments: Management responsibilities expressed in the information security policy.

Control Title: Inventory of information and other associated assets
Control Description: An inventory of information and other associated assets, including owners, shall be developed and maintained
Comments: Ownership model defined; maintained asset inventories not evidenced — see NC-002.

Control Title: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
Control Description: Rules for the acceptable use and procedures for handling information and other associated assets shall be identified, documented and implemented.
Evidence Location: Acceptable Use Policy (NGU-POL-8.1.3) / Information Asset Owners Handbook (NGU-POL-8.1)
Comments: Acceptable use of information and assets defined in the Acceptable Use Policy.

Control Description: Personnel and other interested parties as appropriate shall return all the organisation’s assets in their possession upon change or termination of their employment, contract or agreement.
Comments: Asset return is an HR/offboarding process; not covered by reviewed policies.

Control Description: Information shall be classified according to the information security needs of the organisation based on confidentiality, integrity, availability and relevant interested party requirements.
Comments: Information Classification Policy (4 levels) directly addresses this control.

Control Description: An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organisation.
Comments: Labelling requirements defined within the Information Classification Policy.

Control Description: Information transfer rules, procedures, or agreements shall be in place for all types of transfer facilities within the organisation and between the organisation and other parties.
Comments: Transfer/handling partly covered by the Classification Policy; no dedicated transfer-agreement procedure evidenced.

Control Description: Rules to control physical and logical access to information and other associated assets shall be established and implemented based on business and information security requirements.
Comments: Dedicated Access Control Policy defines access principles, user access management and authentication standards; enforcement gaps tracked in NC-001.

Control Description: The full life cycle of identities shall be managed.
Comments: User registration, provisioning and de-provisioning defined in the Access Control Policy; technical IAM verification still required.

Control Description: Allocation and management of authentication information shall be controlled by a management process, including advising personnel on appropriate handling of authentication information.
Comments: Authentication standards (MFA, password rules) defined; MFA enforcement gap tracked in NC-001.

Control Description: Access rights to information and other associated assets shall be provisioned, reviewed, modified and removed in accordance with the organisation’s topic-specific policy on and rules for access control.
Comments: Provisioning, six-monthly review and removal of access rights defined; records verification still required.

Control Description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the use of supplier’s products or services.
Comments: Supplier risk tiering, due diligence and contractual security requirements defined in the Supplier Security Policy.

Control Title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
Control Description: Relevant information security requirements shall be established and agreed with each supplier based on the type of supplier relationship.
Comments: Security and data-protection clauses for supplier agreements defined in the Supplier Security Policy.

Control Title: Information security for use of cloud services
Control Description: Processes for acquisition, use, management and exit from cloud services shall be established in accordance with the organisation’s information security requirements.
Comments: No cloud-services security policy. New ISO/IEC 27001:2022 control — high-priority transition gap.

Control Title: Information security incident management planning and preparation
Control Description: The organisation shall plan and prepare for managing information security incidents by defining, establishing and communicating information security incident management processes, roles and responsibilities.
Comments: Incident-management responsibilities, lifecycle and severity model defined in the Incident Management Policy.

Control Title: Assessment and decision on information security events
Control Description: The organisation shall assess information security events and decide if they are to be categorised as information security incidents.
Comments: Event assessment and triage (severity classification) defined in the Incident Management Policy.

Control Description: Information security incidents shall be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures.
Comments: Incident response lifecycle (contain, eradicate, recover) defined in the Incident Management Policy.

Control Description: Knowledge gained from information security incidents shall be used to strengthen and improve the information security controls.
Comments: Post-incident review and continual improvement defined in the Incident Management Policy.

Control Description: The organisation shall establish and implement procedures for the identification, collection, acquisition and preservation of evidence related to information security events.
Comments: Evidence identification, collection and chain of custody defined in the Incident Management Policy.

Control Description: The organisation shall plan how to maintain information security at an appropriate level during disruption.
Comments: Information security during disruption not addressed; flag for inclusion in next (2022) assessment.

Control Description: ICT readiness shall be planned, implemented, maintained and tested based on business continuity objectives and ICT continuity requirements.
Comments: ICT readiness for business continuity not covered. New ISO/IEC 27001:2022 control — transition gap.

Control Title: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
Control Description: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements relevant to information security and the organisation’s approach to meet these requirements shall be identified, documented and kept up to date.
Comments: UK GDPR obligations referenced (e.g., special-category data); no consolidated legal/regulatory register evidenced.

Control Description: The organisation’s approach to managing information security and its implementation including people, processes and technologies shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals, or when significant changes occur.
Comments: This tabletop assessment provides a documentation-level independent review; recurring independent-review cadence not evidenced.

Control Title: Compliance with policies, rules and standards for information security
Control Description: Compliance with the organisation’s information security policy, topic-specific policies, rules and standards shall be regularly reviewed.
Comments: Compliance expectations documented; ongoing compliance monitoring limited — see NC-004.

Control Description: Background verification checks on all candidates to become personnel shall be carried out prior to joining the organisation and on an ongoing basis taking into consideration applicable laws, regulations and ethics and be proportional to the business requirements, the classification of the information to be accessed and the perceived risks.
Comments: Personnel screening is an HR-records control; not covered by reviewed policies.

Control Description: The employment contractual agreements shall state the personnel’s and the organisation’s responsibilities for information security.
Comments: Employment terms are an HR control; requires contract verification.

Control Title: Information security awareness, education and training
Control Description: Personnel of the organisation and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate information security awareness, education and training and regular updates of the organisation's information security policy, topic-specific policies and procedures, as relevant for their job function.
Comments: No awareness/training policy in the reviewed set; IAO training recommended (see NC-002).

Control Description: A disciplinary process shall be formalised and communicated to take actions against personnel and other relevant interested parties who have committed an information security policy violation
Comments: Disciplinary process is an HR control.

Control Title: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
Control Description: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment shall be defined, enforced and communicated to relevant personnel and other interested parties.
Comments: Termination/change responsibilities are an HR/IT offboarding control.

Control Description: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting the organisation’s needs for the protection of information shall be identified, documented, regularly reviewed and signed by personnel and other relevant interested parties.
Comments: NDAs/confidentiality agreements are a Legal/HR control; not in reviewed set.

Control Description: Security measures shall be implemented when personnel are working remotely to protect information accessed, processed or stored outside the organisation’s premises.
Comments: Remote Working Policy directly addresses this control; centralised enforcement, MFA and monitoring not evidenced — see NC-001.

Control Description: The organisation shall provide a mechanism for personnel to report observed or suspected information security events through appropriate channels in a timely manner.
Comments: Event-reporting mechanism, channels and 72-hour breach path defined in the Incident Management Policy.

Control Description: Information stored on, processed by or accessible via user end point devices shall be protected.
Evidence Location: Remote Working Policy (NGU-POL-6.2) / Acceptable Use Policy (NGU-POL-8.1.3)
Comments: Endpoint use addressed in Remote Working/AUP; endpoint compliance validation not evidenced — see NC-001.

Control Description: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed.
Comments: Privileged access restricted, approved and logged per the Access Control Policy; technical verification required; relates to NC-001.

Control Description: Access to information and other associated assets shall be restricted in accordance with the established topic-specific policy on access control.
Comments: Information access restriction defined per access-control principles and classification; technical verification required.

Control Description: Read and write access to source code, development tools and software libraries shall be appropriately managed.
Comments: Source-code access relevant only where bespoke development exists.

Control Description: Secure authentication technologies and procedures shall be implemented based on information access restrictions and the topic-specific policy on access control.
Comments: Secure authentication and MFA mandated by the Access Control Policy; enforcement gap tracked in NC-001.

Control Description: The use of resources shall be monitored and adjusted in line with current and expected capacity requirements.
Comments: Capacity management is an operational/technical control.

Control Description: Protection against malware shall be implemented and supported by appropriate user awareness.
Comments: Anti-malware controls require technical verification; endpoint malware risk noted in report (F1).

Control Description: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained, the organisation’s exposure to such vulnerabilities shall be evaluated and appropriate measures shall be taken.
Comments: No patch-management or vulnerability-scanning programme evidenced — high-priority gap (see assessment report).

Control Description: Configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services and networks shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored and reviewed.
Comments: Configuration management not covered. New ISO/IEC 27001:2022 control — transition gap.

Control Description: Information stored in information systems, devices or in any other storage media shall be deleted when no longer required.
Comments: Secure information deletion/retention not addressed; relevant to UK GDPR Art.17. New 2022 control — transition gap.

Control Description: Backup copies of information, software and systems shall be maintained and regularly tested in accordance with the agreed topic-specific policy on backup.
Comments: Backup arrangements require technical verification.

Control Description: Information processing facilities shall be implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements.
Comments: Redundancy/availability is a technical control.

Control Description: Logs that record activities, exceptions, faults and other relevant events shall be produced, stored, protected and analysed.
Implementation Status: Logging may exist; review not evidenced
Comments: No evidence of audit-log review; logging programme not demonstrated — see NC-004.

Control Description: Networks, systems and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents.
Comments: No monitoring activities, KRIs/KCIs or dashboards evidenced. New 2022 control mirroring Finding F4 — see NC-004.

Control Description: The clocks of information processing systems used by the organisation shall be synchronised to approved time sources.
Comments: Clock synchronisation is a technical control.

Control Title: Installation of software on operational systems
Control Description: Procedures and measures shall be implemented to securely manage software installation on operational systems
Comments: Software-installation controls require technical verification (AUP-adjacent).

Control Description: Networks and network devices shall be secured, managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications
Comments: Network security relevant to remote access; centralised enforcement not evidenced — see NC-001.

Control Description: Security mechanisms, service levels and service requirements of network services shall be identified, implemented and monitored.
Comments: Security of network services requires technical verification.

Control Description: Groups of information services, users and information systems shall be segregated in the organisation’s networks.
Comments: Network segregation is a technical control.

Control Description: Access to external websites shall be managed to reduce exposure to malicious content.
Comments: Web filtering not referenced in AUP/Remote Working Policy. New 2022 control — transition gap.

Control Description: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including cryptographic key management, shall be defined and implemented.
Comments: Cryptographic standards and key management defined in the Cryptographic Policy (NGU-POL-10.1).

Control Description: Rules for the secure development of software and systems shall be established and applied.
Comments: Secure SDLC relevant only where bespoke development exists.

Control Description: Information security requirements shall be identified, specified and approved when developing or acquiring applications.
Comments: Application-security requirements relevant where in-house apps are developed.

Control Title: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
Control Description: Principles for engineering secure systems shall be established, documented, maintained and applied to any information system development activities.
Comments: Secure architecture principles relevant to development activity.

Control Description: Secure coding principles shall be applied to software development
Comments: Secure coding applicable only if bespoke software is developed; document applicability in the SoA. New 2022 control.

Control Title: Security testing in development and acceptance
Control Description: Security testing processes shall be defined and implemented in the development life cycle.
Comments: Security testing relevant to development/acceptance pipelines.

Control Title: Separation of development, test and production environments
Control Description: Development, testing and production environments shall be separated and secured.
Comments: Environment separation is a technical/dev-ops control.

Control Title: Protection of information systems during audit testing
Control Description: Audit tests and other assurance activities involving assessment of operational systems shall be planned and agreed between the tester and appropriate management.
Comments: Protection during audit testing requires process verification.
